subject: History
chapter: Mahatma Gandhi and the Nationalist Movement

formulas.name: Gandhian Non-Violence
formulas.variables: A concept that emphasizes the use of non-violent methods to achieve social and political change
formulas.notes: Gandhi's core philosophy that guides his approach to politics and social reform

formulas.name: Satyagraha
formulas.variables: A concept that combines truth and firmness to achieve social and political change
formulas.notes: A key strategy used by Gandhi to achieve social and political change

formulas.name: Swadeshi Movement
formulas.formula: Use Indian-made goods to promote economic self-sufficiency
formulas.variables: A movement that emphasizes the use of Indian-made goods to promote economic self-sufficiency
formulas.notes: A key aspect of the Indian nationalist movement that aims to promote Indian industries and reduce dependence on foreign goods

formulas.name: Non-Cooperation Movement
formulas.variables: A movement that involves boycotting British goods and institutions to achieve social and political change
formulas.notes: A key strategy used by Gandhi to achieve social and political change, which involved boycotting British goods and institutions

formulas.name: Quit India Movement
formulas.variables: A movement that demands the British to leave India immediately
formulas.notes: A key movement that emerged during the Second World War, demanding the British to leave India immediately

formulas.name: Ahimsa
formulas.variables: A concept that emphasizes the importance of non-violence and compassion in achieving social and political change
formulas.notes: A key aspect of Gandhi's philosophy that emphasizes the importance of non-violence and compassion
